headline: Airfares skyrocket as much as 218% on Spirit Airlines’ busiest routes within just 48 hours of its May 2 liquidation
summary: A round-trip ticket from Las Vegas to Dallas-Fort Worth cost $39 on Spirit Airlines the morning of May 1. By the afternoon of May 3, that same itinerary on the next-cheapest carrier was selling for $124. That’s a 217.9% jump, and it happened inside the 48-hour window after Spirit’s 3:00 AM ET shutdown on May ... Airfares skyrocket as much as 218% on Spirit Airlines’ busiest routes within just 48 hours of its May 2 liquidation

headline: UBS Just Hiked Delta Air Lines Price Target to $95: Premium Cabin Strategy Targets $13 EPS
summary: UBS just turned more bullish on one of the airline industry’s premium-strategy poster children. The firm raised its price target on Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) stock to $95 from $86 and reiterated a Buy rating, pointing to a margin and earnings trajectory that looks structurally different from past airline cycles. headline: US Airlines Spent $5 Billion On Fuel In March Alone As Energy Crisis Deepens: Here's How Airline Stocks Have Fared Since Iran War Began
summary: Airline fuel costs surged 56% in March to over $5 billion due to war with Iran and oil market disruption, according to fresh BTS data.
